claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO organises nine WPs in three structural layers (research pillars WP2/ATU, WP3/BIIS, WP4/CERIA; demonstrators WP5/NIHS, WP6/ISIA, WP7/ELI; cross-cutting support WP1/ATU, WP8/FIIT, WP9/BAL)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — work_packages[].wp_id and work_packages[].lead_partner for all nine WPs confirmed present and consistent with stated layer structure; partner_role_matrix[].wps_as_lead confirms each lead assignment

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The dependency map contains 16 confirmed inter-WP data-input edges forming an acyclic graph
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — dependency_map.edges array contains exactly 16 entries, all with edge_type 'data_input'; cycle_detected: false confirms acyclic structure; cycle_check_method: Kahn's topological sort confirms DAG

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: The three research pillar WPs (WP2, WP3, WP4) feed the three demonstrator WPs (WP5, WP6, WP7) through 9 pillar-to-demonstrator edges
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — dependency_map.edges: WP2→WP5, WP2→WP6, WP2→WP7, WP3→WP5, WP3→WP6, WP3→WP7, WP4→WP5, WP4→WP6, WP4→WP7 — nine edges confirmed, all edge_type 'data_input'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Each demonstrator WP feeds WP8 (3 edges: WP5→WP8, WP6→WP8, WP7→WP8); WP8 feeds WP9 (1 edge)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — dependency_map.edges: WP5→WP8, WP6→WP8, WP7→WP8 (3 edges) and WP8→WP9 (1 edge) all confirmed present with edge_type 'data_input'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Critical path runs WP2→WP5→WP8→WP9
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — critical_path_nodes: [WP2, WP5, WP8, WP9]; critical_path_analysis.representative_path_selection_rationale confirms WP2→WP5→WP8→WP9 as representative of nine tied paths of equal length (3 edges); corroborated by docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase4_gantt_milestones/gantt.json — critical_path: [T2-01, T2-02, T2-03, T5-01, T5-02, T5-03, T8-03] traversing WP2→WP5→WP8

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Risk register classifies 11 risks; four high-impact risks identified: RISK-01 (neuro-symbolic integration, medium/high) via modular fallback architecture; RISK-04 (clinical data access, medium/high) via parallel public-dataset strategy and early ethics submission; RISK-09 (partner commitment, low/high) via deputy PI appointments; RISK-10 (framework integration, medium/high) via shared API contracts M1–M3
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — risk_register array contains exactly 11 entries (RISK-01 through RISK-11); RISK-01: likelihood=medium impact=high mitigation includes 'modular architecture' and 'fallback modes'; RISK-04: likelihood=medium impact=high mitigation includes 'publicly available clinical datasets (MIMIC-IV, eICU)' and 'Submit ethics applications in M1'; RISK-09: likelihood=low impact=high mitigation includes 'deputy PI appointments'; RISK-10: likelihood=medium impact=high mitigation includes 'API contracts' in 'M1–M3'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: RISK-07 (LLM landscape change) has high likelihood and is mitigated via model-agnostic interfaces and annual re-evaluation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json — risk_register RISK-07: likelihood=high; mitigation states 'model-agnostic interfaces that can integrate new LLMs without architectural changes' and 'annual baseline re-evaluation (M12, M24, M36) against latest models'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Consortium combines academic technical excellence, applied research capacity, and private sector deployment expertise across the full value chain from foundational AI research to operational validation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json — organisation_type fields establish mix: HES (ATU, BIIS, ISIA, NIHS — academic/higher education), REC (CERIA, FIIT — research centres), PRC (ELI, BAL — private sector); claim that this mix constitutes 'academic technical excellence, applied research capacity, and private sector deployment expertise across the full value chain' is a logical characterisation derived from the documented organisation type diversity. Derivation chain: HES partners → academic technical excellence; REC partners → applied research capacity; PRC partners → private sector deployment expertise; combined scope from foundational research (WP2-4) to operational validation (WP5-7) follows from wp_structure.json WP layer design.
